001/**
002 * A beamer is a special item that can be charged and fired.
003 * When charged, it memorizes the current room.
004 * When fired, it teleports the player back to that room.
005 *
006 * @author Raphaël Quillaud
007 * @version 2026.05.09
008 */
009public class Beamer extends Item {
010    private Room aChargedRoom;
011
012    /**
013     * Constructor for Beamer.
014     * @param pName the name of the beamer
015     * @param pDescription the description of the beamer
016     * @param pWeight the weight of the beamer
017     */
018    public Beamer(final String pName, final String pDescription, final int pWeight) {
019        super(pName, pDescription, pWeight);
020        this.aChargedRoom = null;
021    }
022
023    /**
024     * Charges the beamer with the given room.
025     * @param pRoom the room to memorize
026     */
027    public void charge(final Room pRoom) {
028        this.aChargedRoom = pRoom;
029    }
030
031    /**
032     * Returns true if the beamer is charged.
033     */
034    public boolean isCharged() {
035        return this.aChargedRoom != null;
036    }
037
038    /**
039     * Fires the beamer and returns the memorized room.
040     * The beamer is reset after firing.
041     * Returns null if not charged.
042     */
043    public Room fire() {
044        Room vRoom = this.aChargedRoom;
045        this.aChargedRoom = null;
046        return vRoom;
047    }
048} // Beamer
